title: aglaia teysmanniana
intro: Aglaia teysmanniana, also known as the Teysmann's aglaia, is a species of tree in the family Meliaceae, native to the tropical forests of Indonesia and Malaysia. The bark and seeds of the Aglaia teysmanniana plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine, exhibiting medicinal properties such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Aglaia teysmanniana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, while its extracts have also shown antioxidant and cytotoxic properties. Aglaia teysmanniana can be prepared into herbal remedies through various methods, including decoction, infusion, and extraction, which are used to create traditional remedies for various ailments.
